title: UNIX Signal Handling Flow for a Daemon Process
description: A flowchart depicting the lifecycle of a daemon process, focusing on its initialization, main loop, and the handling of common signals like SIGHUP, SIGTERM, and SIGCHLD.
relationships: Sequential flow from start to main loop
Signal interrupts main loop and triggers handler
Handler determines action based on signal type (e.g., reload config, terminate, reap child)
Process either returns to main loop or proceeds to cleanup
